methodology.logic: Represents a single anchor in the lineage graph

H-05.1 Circular Lineage Detector

Uses Tarjan's algorithm for strongly connected components to detect
circular dependencies in agent lineage graphs.

Attack Scenario:
Agent A creates Anchor α linking to Agent B's Anchor β
Agent B creates Anchor β linking back to Agent A's Anchor α
Result: Circular endorsement that artificially inflates reputation

Defense:
Graph traversal detects cycles before settlement
Any anchor that creates a cycle is BLOCKED

Test: Simple linear chain (no cycle)
Expected: PASS

Test: Two agents create mutual lineage loop
Expected: BLOCKED

Attack Scenario:
Agent A creates Anchor α
Agent B creates Anchor β linking to α
Agent A creates Anchor α2 linking to β
Complete cycle: β links back to α2 (CREATES CYCLE)

Test: Agent creates cycle within own anchors
Expected: BLOCKED

Attack Scenario:
Agent A creates chain: α1 → α2 → α3 → α1

Test: Complex cycle involving 4 agents
Expected: BLOCKED

Attack Scenario:
A → B → C → D → A (4-agent circular endorsement)

Test: Multiple separate cycles in graph
Expected: BLOCKED (all cycles detected)

Attack Scenario:
Cycle 1: A ↔ B
Cycle 2: C ↔ D

Test: The "Ouroboros" attack - perfect circular chain
Expected: BLOCKED

Named Attack: The snake eating its tail
Description: Chain of agents each endorsing the next,
             with the last endorsing the first
